title: Small Language Models Take Over Production Workloads
subtitle: Sub-10B models now carry the bulk of shipped inference
metaDescription: Production inference is consolidating on small language models.
heroImageAlt: Low-poly illustration of an open backpack holding a laptop and chips, a faceted cathedral city rising from it
bodyHtml:
Production AI workloads are consolidating on small language models, with sub-10B systems now handling the majority of shipped inference across classification, extraction, and routing tasks. Teams report cost reductions of an order of magnitude against frontier-model baselines with no measurable quality loss on scoped tasks.

The shift follows a now-standard pattern: prototype on the largest available model, define the task envelope, then distill until the quality curve breaks. Deployment increasingly lands on-device or in-VPC, removing the data-residency objections that stalled enterprise rollouts through 2024.
pullQuote: Prototype big, measure the task, distill until quality breaks.
whyItMatters: The economics of shipped AI are being set by small models, not frontier ones, and pricing pressure flows downhill from there.
